steps.title: Spot the main verb
steps.content: The comma before “assembles” reveals that “assembles” is the main verb. The blank precedes this comma, so it must introduce a modifier to Richard Serra, not another independent clause.

steps.title: Match the punctuation rule
steps.content:
The sentence expresses both Serra's intent and his action. Using “intending his installations to make passersby keenly aware...” as a participle phrase—set off by commas—achieves this with “assembles” as the main verb.

The rule is: A participle phrase in the middle of a sentence must be set off by commas on both sides.

Options A and D create comma splices; option B misplaces a comma between the subject and verb.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
